text:
Anushka Patil and Rebecca F. Elliott contributed reporting.
Abbas Araghchi, the foreign minister of Iran, told CBS News on Sunday that the country was not ready to negotiate with the United States, despite President Trump saying the previous day that Iran wanted to make a deal. “We never asked for a cease fire, and we have never asked even for negotiation,” Araghchi told “Face the Nation.” “We are ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes.” Trump said in a phone interview with NBC News on Saturday that “Iran wants to make a deal, and I don’t want to make it because the terms aren’t good enough yet.”
The Israeli military said on Sunday that the brother of the man who drove a truck into a synagogue in Michigan last Thursday was a Hezbollah commander who was killed in an Israeli airstrike in Lebanon earlier this month.
title: Trump adds to speculation about Iranian leader’s condition.
